name: thalamus
description: Thalamus — relay and gating between cortical columns. Every cortical L5→L4 connection passes through a thalamic relay. GPi controls relay state (inhibited by default, disinhibited by Go pathway). Reticular nucleus creates competition between relays. Includes dedicated relays for PFC stripes, operation columns, Broca's area (VA nucleus), and temporal cortex.

nodes.description: Thalamic relay for PFC stripe 0. Passes signals from operation columns to PFC WM0 when disinhibited by GPi.
Relay for addition column output. Routes add results to PFC.
Ventral anterior (VA) thalamic relay for Broca's area. Disinhibited by BG stripe 3, signals BA44 that Merge is permitted. This is the return path of the Broca cortico-BG-thalamocortical loop.
Thalamic relay for temporal cortex. Routes input column signals to Wernicke's phonological buffer.
Thalamic reticular nucleus for stripe 0. Inhibitory — creates competition between relays. When relay_0 is active, TRN_0 inhibits neighboring relays.

edges.description: Active relay excites its reticular neuron.
Reticular inhibition: active relay suppresses neighbors.
Broca TRN weakly inhibits PFC relays — Merge processing competes with WM updating.
Temporal TRN weakly inhibits Broca relay — receiving new tokens slightly suppresses ongoing Merge.

properties.default_state: Relays are inhibited by GPi (via connections specified in config.json). When Go pathway disinhibits a relay, signals pass through to the target cortex.
properties.reticular_function: The TRN creates winner-take-all competition. When one relay opens, it suppresses the others. This focuses attention — only the most relevant input reaches its target at any given moment.
properties.va_nucleus: relay_broca is the VA (ventral anterior) thalamic relay, biologically distinct from VL (ventral lateral, motor). It carries the return signal of the Broca cortico-BG-thalamocortical loop.
